rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF JANAINA MOREIRA-KANALEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/23/21. (No earlier date available.) The verification of the petition is incomplete. The First Republic statement is for the period ending 10/31/20. No reconciliation is shown for the cash balance shown in the accounting and the balance shown in the statement. The number of shares are missing from Schedule F. Should there not be a schedule of receivables, given the failure of the mother to continue monthly payments after March 2019? (Paragraph 7b of the petition is unclear as to when and to whom the application for waiver of penalties and interest was made that resulted in the monthly payments. No dates or case numbers, etc., are set forth.) The time entries on 9/16/19 are not also reflected in the attorney's time sheets. The account does not reflect a second payment of costs to the attorney on 5/11/19. On the other hand, there was no additional bond filed on 5/8/19. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner no later than 3/12/21.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LOUISE TSCHUDI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/16/21. (No earlier date available.) Proof of the additional bond must be filed before an account can be approved. An appearance will be required on 3/16/21 (after proof of additional bond is filed). (A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct information. [CRC 7.3.] Examiner's Notes are not filed for the record and should not be "responded" to. Each filed pleading should stand on its own and not refer to Examiner's Notes. A "response" in a "Declaration" without any context makes no sense, and a "response" that something was explained in the last accounting, when the last accounting is not currently before the Court and did not, in fact, address the current issue about an insufficiently described refund in the current account is inadequate to supplement the missing information in the current account.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET BOYD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/23/21. (No earlier date available.) The accounting period exceeds the two-year accounting period permitted by this Court for a first account. [LRSF 14.35 A.] The schedules do not have the dates for each transaction and do not identify the payees or set forth the full description for each disbursement. The informational schedule (changes in the form of assets) also appears to be incomplete; there is no description of the nature of the changes other than "Shares Received" (although not every transaction resulted in shares). The I&A does not appear to have been signed by all three co-executors. No Corrected I&A has been filed yet. Original financial statements have not been filed to verify the assets on hand. [LRSF. 14.35 D.] The attorney's or fiduciary's declaration (whoever retains the original(s)) must also be filed, certifying that the original statement(s) will be retained as set forth in LRSF 2.11 M.4. No allowance or rejection of any creditor's claim has been filed. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] Not all claims appear to have been paid. The attorney's costs must also be itemized, showing the date, payee, description, and amount for each transaction, not lump sums. Why are the co-executors, who are also the sole beneficiaries, as the co-trustees of the devisee trust, accounting? It appears that the accounting will need to be amended to be a two-year accounting or the petition must be amended if accounting will be waived. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner no later than 3/12/21.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JULES HAYWOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/2021. Issues: The petition requests authorization to distribute $52,866.13 to the San Francisco Public Administrator pursuant to Probate Code 7660. The proposed order requests a different amount $53,301.13 be distributed. Decedent's estate exceeds $50,000.00 and petitioner must petition by ex parte. Probate Code 7660(a)(1) The authority provided by this paragraph may be exercised only upon order of the court. The order may be made upon ex parte application. Probate Code 7660 (a)(2) The total value of the property in the decedents estate does not exceed fifty thousand dollars ($50,000).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUANNE L. CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/10/2021 Issues (same issues as prior notes no filed response): 1) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 L. 2)] 2) The petition alleges administrator's counsel will not seek an order for compensation. Please indicate if administrator's counsel is waiving compensation or will be paid outside of the estate. If attorney will be paid outside of the estate please indicate the amount as it cannot exceed the statutory amount. Please indicate if the attorney has already been paid. If the attorney had already been paid see CRC 7.700. The personal representative must neither pay nor receive, and the attorney for the personal representative must not receive, statutory commissions or fees or fees for extraordinary services in advance of an order of the court authorizing their payment.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) The temporary conservator included social security benefits payable to the conservatee's son in the assets of this conservatorship and took no action to segregate the benefits that did not belong to the conservatee; and 2) The temporary conservator's attorney is requesting fees of $26,097.50 including fees for her services to the general conservator whose first account has not been filed and who has not filed any petition in which the attorney can join. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

An appearance will be required on 3/16/21 after proof of additional bond is filed in the related case. (A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct information. [CRC 7.3.] Examiner's Notes are not filed for the record and should not be "responded" to. Each filed pleading should stand on its own and not refer to Examiner's Notes. A "response" in a "Declaration" without any context makes no sense.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GARY R. PRICE JR. AND SHERRY H.L. PRICE 1997 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Approve Trust Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2021. 1) Direct notice must be given to Sherry Price, absent a written directive signed by Sherry Price that notice to her attorney is sufficient. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). 2) The petition must state the names and addresses of each person entitled to notice of the petition. Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902. 3) If an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three years and each three-year period must be filed separately. LRSF 14.35(A).

Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Examiner notes requested an explanation if the Will is an original. [See LRSF 14.20, LRSF 14.23.] The 01/22/2021 Declaration of attorney Ritzi Lam alleges Petitioner confirmed the lodged Will is an original.
